assumptions: Assume the question concerns the consolidated financial statements.
Assume the intragroup dividend receivable creates foreign currency exposure because it is denominated in a currency different from a relevant functional currency.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a fair value hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposure to changes in the fair value of a recognised asset or liability attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions, including specified intragroup foreign currency exposures in consolidation.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify a net investment hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk arising from a net investment in a foreign operation in consolidated financial statements.
